claims: Text edges out images at top rank, but images match or exceed text at deeper recall levels.
Neither approach alone is necessarily better; they fail on different queries.
Multimodal hybrid search gives the best of both worlds.
core_facts: Tested retrieval over 3,230 pages from 166 scientific papers using 180 needle-in-the-haystack queries.
Text-based retrieval (Arctic 2.0 + BM25 hybrid search): 46% Recall@1, 78% Recall@5, 91% Recall@20.
Image-based retrieval (ColModernVBERT multi-vector embeddings): 43% Recall@1, 78% Recall@5, 93% Recall@20.
Multimodal hybrid search: 49% Recall@1, 81% Recall@5, 95% Recall@20.
At Recall@1, 22 queries succeeded with text but failed with images; 18 queries succeeded with images but failed with text.
event_type: Weaviate publishes comparison of text vs image retrieval for RAG
summary: Weaviate shares results comparing text-based retrieval (Arctic 2.0+BM25) and image-based retrieval (ColModernVBERT) for RAG on PDFs. Text achieves 46%/78%/91% Recall@1/@5/@20, image achieves 43%/78%/93%, and multimodal hybrid achieves 49%/81%/95%. They find the methods fail on different queries, suggesting fusion is best.
